hero.title: Center of Islamic Civilization — Abu Dhabi

sections.title: Our Heritage
sections.body: Founded in the heart of Abu Dhabi, the Center of Islamic Civilization has dedicated over three decades to preserving and teaching the ancient art of Arabic calligraphy — one of Islam's most revered visual traditions.

sections.title: The Styles We Teach
sections.body: From the geometric precision of Kufic to the flowing curves of Naskh, Thuluth, Diwani, and Riq'a — our master calligraphers guide students through every major script tradition.

sections.title: Who Can Join
sections.body: Beginners, students, and seasoned artists are all welcome. Courses run in Arabic and English, with flexible scheduling for in-person and online learners worldwide.

sections.title: Our Mission
sections.body: We believe calligraphy is not merely decoration — it is a meditative practice and a living language. Our mission: to carry this sacred script into the next generation.

courses.description: From the first stroke to monumental inscription — find the course that matches your journey.
courses.empty: No courses available at this level yet.

cards.desc: The most widely used script in the Arab world. Learn the foundational letterforms, proportions, and ink techniques used by scribes for centuries.
One of the most majestic scripts in Islamic art. Master the sweeping curves and architectural proportions that adorn mosques and manuscripts worldwide.
Developed in the Ottoman imperial court, Diwani is celebrated for its flowing, ornate style. A script of elegance and prestige.
The earliest Arabic script — angular, geometric, and deeply spiritual. Study the ancient forms found in the oldest copies of the Quran.
The everyday script of the Arab world — fast, clear, and elegant. Learn the cursive style used in daily handwriting across the Arab world.
The pinnacle of Arabic calligraphy. Jali Thuluth is used for monumental inscriptions on mosques and palaces. Reserved for dedicated students.

about.p1: Founded under the patronage of the Center of Islamic Civilization in 1989, our Calligraphy Center stands as one of the Arab world's foremost institutions dedicated to the study, preservation, and advancement of classical Arabic and Islamic scripts.
about.p2: Over three decades, we have trained more than 4,000 students from 60 nations, maintained a living archive of over 2,000 historical manuscripts, and nurtured a faculty of internationally recognized master calligraphers.
